description: Verify driver authentication credentials before first delivery
summary: Tennessee HB-1414 requires gig delivery platforms to verify driver eligibility through three forms of clearance before allowing first delivery. This includes government-issued ID verification, background check, and vehicle registration confirmation. The current grace period for new Driver Partners must be eliminated.
in_scope: All new Driver Partners in Tennessee
Gopuff app and website onboarding flows

requirements.current_state: Gopuff collects driver name and email during onboarding. No government ID is verified.
requirements.gap_risk: Non-compliant — missing government ID verification entirely
requirements.future_state: Driver Ops: Collect government-issued ID (driver license or passport) and verify against issuing authority before account activation

requirements.current_state: Background checks run asynchronously after Driver Partner acceptance; clearance does not block first delivery.
requirements.gap_risk: Non-compliant — timing and enforcement do not align with statute requirement
requirements.future_state: Engineering: Gate first delivery on successful background check completion. Block delivery if background check fails or pending.

requirements.current_state: We collect vehicle description (year, make, model) but do not verify registration status with DMV.
requirements.gap_risk: Partial — we have vehicle data but no registration verification
requirements.future_state: Engineering: Integrate with Tennessee DMV API to verify vehicle registration at onboarding. Reject unregistered vehicles.

requirements.current_state: New Driver Partners can make 5 deliveries before background check clears (grace period).
requirements.gap_risk: Non-compliant — statute prohibits any deliveries before all three clearances complete
requirements.future_state: Driver Ops: Remove existing 5-delivery grace period. Gate first TN delivery on all three clearances completing.

assumptions: Background check turnaround time is <2 hours for 95% of applications
Tennessee DMV API is available and responsive
Driver Partners can resubmit failed background checks
Vehicle registration verification covers all Tennessee-registered vehicles
open_questions: If Driver Partner's background check fails, can they reapply immediately or after a waiting period?
What happens to in-flight deliveries if a Driver Partner's registration is revoked mid-shift?
Are we liable if DMV API returns incorrect verification results?
Should we grandfather existing Driver Partners or apply rule retroactively?
